layout.pageDescription: Thirdspace at the University of Toronto. A research group studying human-computer interaction, AI ethics, and communities at the margins.

hero.subParagraph: Building socio-technical systems for responsible, participatory, and context-aware AI with communities too often left outside the room where technology is designed.
hero.title: Utilizing human values, situated knowledge, and experiences for developing impactful solutions.
hero.description: The Third Space research group, led by Prof. Ishtiaque Ahmed at the University of Toronto, studies the social, ethical, and political dimensions of computing and artificial intelligence.
hero.researchPostureBody: Technology is never neutral; we study who it serves, who it misses, and how it can be remade.

groupOverview.headline: Computing research from the social edge inward.
groupOverview.body: The Third Space research group, led by Prof. Ishtiaque Ahmed at the University of Toronto, studies the social, ethical, and political dimensions of computing and artificial intelligence. The group works at the intersection of human-computer interaction (HCI), AI, and critical computing to understand how technologies shape—and are shaped by—everyday life practices, particularly in contexts of marginalization and global development, centering communities that are often overlooked in mainstream computing research.

about.title: About the Group
about.body: Through empirical and design-oriented research, the group develops and studies socio-technical systems that support more responsible, participatory, and context-aware forms of AI and digital technologies. Their work is grounded in collaboration with communities and draws on interdisciplinary perspectives to reimagine how computing systems can better serve diverse human values and needs.

team.pageBody: The Thirdspace group brings together faculty, postdocs, graduate students, and undergraduates who study the social, ethical, and political dimensions of computing and AI.
team.emptyAlumniMessage: List will be updated as graduates move on

aboutPage.headline: Studying computing from the margins inward.
aboutPage.subhead: Founded and led by Prof. Ishtiaque Ahmed at the University of Toronto, Thirdspace is a research group that studies the social, ethical, and political dimensions of computing and artificial intelligence.
aboutPage.heroSubParagraph: Thirdspace is a research collective at the University of Toronto reimagining how AI and digital systems can be designed alongside — not for — communities shaped by marginalization, displacement, and grassroots care.
aboutPage.heroResearchPostureBody: Critical, empirical, and participatory — we treat every technology as a question of power, and every design as an act of accountability.
aboutPage.missionBody: Through empirical and design-oriented research, we develop and study socio-technical systems that support more responsible, participatory, and context-aware forms of AI and digital technologies — particularly with communities too often left outside the room where technology is designed.
aboutPage.approachBody: We blend long-term fieldwork with iterative system design, partnering with community organizations, public institutions, and grassroots groups in the Global South and at the Canadian margins. Our methods combine ethnography, interviews, co-design workshops, and the building of working prototypes.

approachItems.title: Empirical inquiry
approachItems.body: Ethnography, interviews, and field studies grounded in everyday life practices.

approachItems.title: Participatory design
approachItems.body: Co-design with communities as collaborators — not subjects — of the technologies we build.

approachItems.title: Critical computing
approachItems.body: Questioning the political, ethical, and social stakes of AI and digital infrastructure.

aboutPage.storyBody: Thirdspace sits at the intersection of human-computer interaction, AI, and critical computing. The group is housed in the Department of Computer Science at the University of Toronto and is affiliated with several cross-disciplinary labs and initiatives across the university. Our work has been supported by the Social Sciences and Humanities Research Council of Canada (SSHRC), the National Sciences and Engineering Research Council of Canada (NSERC), the Canadian Institute for Advanced Research (CIFAR), and partner organizations across the Global South.

contact.headline: We'd love to hear from you.
contact.body: Whether you're a prospective student, collaborator, journalist, or community partner — please reach out. We aim to respond within a few working days.
contact.connectBody: We welcome inquiries from prospective graduate students, visiting researchers, and community partners. Due to the volume of requests, we may not be able to reply to every message, but we read each one. For graduate admissions, please apply through the University of Toronto's Department of Computer Science and mention Prof. Ahmed's group in your statement of interest.
